---
title: "Getting Started with scLean"
author: "rzyang-dev"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Getting Started with scLean}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```r
library(scLean)
library(Seurat)
```

## Overview

scLean is a memory-efficient single-cell RNA-seq analysis package that replaces
Seurat's in-memory computation with HDF5-backed, out-of-core algorithms. It is
designed for large datasets (500K+ cells) on machines with limited RAM (8 GB).

The API is identical to standard Seurat — you can use `NormalizeData()`,
`ScaleData()`, `RunPCA()`, and all other Seurat functions as you normally would.

## Loading Data

### From a 10X Genomics directory

```r
pbmc <- LoadScleanObject("path/to/filtered_feature_bc_matrix")
```

### From an existing Seurat object

```r
pbmc <- as.scLean(seurat_obj, hdf5_path = "my_data.h5")
```

### From a dgCMatrix

```r
counts <- Matrix::rsparsematrix(2000, 500, density = 0.1)
pbmc <- LoadScleanObject(counts, assay = "RNA")
```

## Standard Seurat Pipeline

All functions work the same way as standard Seurat, but computation is HDF5-backed:

```r
# Normalize
pbmc <- NormalizeData(pbmc, normalization.method = "LogNormalize")

# Find variable features
pbmc <- FindVariableFeatures(pbmc, nfeatures = 2000)

# Scale
pbmc <- ScaleData(pbmc)

# Run PCA
pbmc <- RunPCA(pbmc, npcs = 30)

# Find neighbors and clusters
pbmc <- FindNeighbors(pbmc, dims = 1:30)
pbmc <- FindClusters(pbmc, resolution = 0.8, algorithm = "leiden")

# Run UMAP (delegates to Seurat's implementation)
pbmc <- RunUMAP(pbmc, dims = 1:30)

# Visualize
DimPlot(pbmc, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE)
```

## Finding Markers

```r
# Differential expression between two clusters
markers <- FindMarkers(pbmc, ident.1 = "1", ident.2 = "2")

# All cluster markers
all_markers <- FindAllMarkers(pbmc)

# View top markers
head(markers[order(markers$p_val_adj), ], n = 10)
```

## Integration

```r
# Split by batch and integrate
obj_list <- SplitObject(pbmc, split.by = "orig.ident")
pbmc <- IntegrateLayers(pbmc, method = "CCAIntegration")
```

## Memory Management

Control how much RAM scLean uses:

```r
# Limit to 4 GB
SetMaxRAM(4096)

# Set a fixed chunk size (5000 cells per chunk)
SetChunkSize(pbmc, 5000)

# Check current memory usage
MemoryReport(pbmc)

# Restore automatic chunk sizing
SetChunkSize(pbmc, NA)
```

## Converting Back to In-Memory Seurat

If you need a standard Seurat object (e.g., for saving as `.rds`):

```r
# Load only the normalized data into memory
pbmc_mem <- as.Seurat.scLean(pbmc, layers = "data")

# Save as standard Seurat RDS
saveRDS(pbmc_mem, file = "pbmc_seurat.rds")
```

## Saving and Loading scLean Objects

scLean objects store only metadata and the HDF5 path in memory, so they are very
small in RAM. Just make sure the HDF5 file is in the same location when loading:

```r
# Save (only metadata + HDF5 path reference)
saveRDS(pbmc, file = "pbmc_sclean.rds")

# Load (HDF5 file must still exist at the original path)
pbmc <- readRDS("pbmc_sclean.rds")
```

## Session Info

```{r}
sessionInfo()
```
